subject: An arranged circle of open folded scrolls and tent-fold books inscribed with glowing blue script and illustrations, displayed on a wooden surface alongside a small teapot and a single cup
description: A still-life arrangement on a worn wooden tabletop. A series of folded scrolls or accordion-bound books, each propped open in a tent shape, are placed in a curving line that spirals toward the viewer. Their pages glow with luminous blue calligraphy and small inked illustrations — sea creatures, dragons, and runic glyphs — that cast spectral light onto the wood around them. The bottom-most book is closed and decorated with an ornate blue-and-gold geometric pattern. To one side sits a small dark-glazed teapot and a matching cup with a stylized blossom motif. No figure is depicted; the scene reads as a private moment of study or storytelling, the texts themselves taking center stage.
foreground: an ornately decorated closed book with blue-and-gold geometric pattern, sitting at the near edge of the wooden table
background: wooden tabletop receding into shadow, scattered with the glowing reflected light of the open scrolls
emotion: contemplative stillness; the absence of a figure invites the viewer into the scholar's seat
